review_text: Outstanding place to stay, very clean, excellent location, Simon an Di were lovely hosts they put on live music during the Easter an Christmas holidays, the best caravan park in Tasmania

We had a great stay at the park. Fabulous for couples and families. We had a powered site and arrived in the rain. We were given a generously sized gravel site. The owners have obviously put a lot of thought into providing a lovely park. New laundry facilities, great camp kitchen  with new bbq, very spacious amenities that were a bit dated but very clean and kids playground. The cabins looked fabulous. We had a camper trailer and being 15 min from the Spirit of Tasmania, we paid a bit more to stay until late afternoon so we could board the boat at night. This was so convenient and the owners were very happy to accommodate our needs. IGA supermarket up the road and such a quiet and relaxing park to stay in that is still very close to Devonport. Try this one out, you won’t be disappointed.
Lovely caravan park with very friendly and helpful owners
Beautiful place

Such a peaceful setting.  Very close to Devonport and Latrobe.  The arboretum is well worth a visit if you want to see platypus.
We thoroughly enjoyed the live entertainment on Sunday night

Just spent the November long weekend at Lakeside..
I was with a group of Snowy River Caravan Club Members ...this was my first Muster.
I found the managers so friendly,the Ammenities were so very clean,as was the Camp Kitchen and Laundry.
Sunday Night we had live music with Wayne Cox..fantastic entertainment.
Would thoroughly recommend.

Lovely peaceful location, quiet area but still reasonably close to shops and amenities.
Simon and Di are great to deal with and the small unit we are staying in is very nice and has pretty much all you need 😀
Nice

After being in Tassie for 11 mths, we definitely fluked leaving one of the best caravan parks for last. The peace and wildlife here convinced us to extend out 2 week stay to over 4 weeks.
The new owners, Simon and Dianne, have been very friendly and welcoming. They have been steadily improving the park over the past few years, renovating the amentities (ongoing), removing dangerous trees, landscaping, upgrading the road system and adding several luxury cabins. Not saying it's as spick and span as some fancier parks we've been in, but we prefer the grass, river, platypus and pademelons over concrete and bitumen of higher profile caravan parks anyhow.
We like that the owners have a reasonable curfew on noise at night also. Not so strick you can't enjoy your time with friends here, but also allows us older ones to get to sleep at a decent hour for us also.
It's about 15 mins into Devonport and there's even an IGA only about 5 mins drive away.
Without doubt, our future visits to Tasmania will likely start and end here in the Lakeside Tourist Caravan Park at Eugenana.

Great place to stay among the nature and the host are wonderful and friendly people.
Great facility in the kitchen area and toilet and showers are well maintained

This place should be a model for caravan parks. The services are excellent and the place is a bit in the middle of nowhere a bit but saying that 15 minutes from big towns in most directions. If we had known about it earlier we would have been here more often. Great camp kitchen but unfortunately the park had backpackers. They tend to not play by rules. We have had experience with this before. Backpackers will soon ruin the chance to get into these places because all the owners we speak to have had enough.

Great park we will definitely return.
Great location, plenty of space, great amenities, accommodating hosts. What's not to like.

Wow. We really enjoyed the serenity of the park but still close to SOT and town if required. Lovely animals roaming happily with guests. Welcoming, helpful friendly owners (Simon and Dianne). Amenities very cleanand smell fresh with music. Laundry and kitchen fantastic. Water was hot. Can't wait to visit again.
Awesome place to stay with family. I took a few good images.

Very nice owners. We rocked up in August with no booking, was fairly quiet so there was a number of powered sites to choose from. Clean amenities with everything you need. free wifi worked well. We saw 2 platypus swimming around in the nearby pond, which was a highlight for us.
Perfect remote getaway while still being only 10 minutes from shops and supermarkets. Clean and tidy amenities

I lived there for 3 months. It's a beautiful caravan park with good facilities, really clean. The cabins are modern and cosy. There is the arboretum just behind, 5 minutes by walk. During the summer you can also go into the river for a swim. There is a lot of nature.
Thank you to Dianne and Simon for everything. Take care, hope to see you again 🌺🌼
Lovely caravan park. Peaceful. Platypus to watch. Very clean facilities. Friendly helpful staff

Beautiful location next to the Arboretum with the creek and Platypus. Large sites.
Excellent amenities, 5 star.
All of this just 15 minutes outside Devonport.
The friendly owners and staff welcomed us at 7.30am after arriving in Tasmania on the ferry.
Thanks for making our stay a memorable one.

This caravan park is a definite must to stay at. Location is fantastic, bush setting is absolutely lovely, amenities are top notch. Owners Simon & Di are extremely hospitable nothing is to much trouble, if they can help you they will . Don’t go passed Lakeside Caravan Park location in Eugenana 10 minutes from  Devonport.
Beautiful park excellent amenities and so clean Beautiful caravan park
Came in very late from Ferry owners were more than hospitable great park next to Tasmania Arboretum creeks rivers ponds Platypus and other wildlife about would stay again 5 nights not long enough

Peaceful country campground near Devonport.  Owners moderately friendly.
Situated near a lake with no access, which was a mine.  No shops or services nearby except a winery that has live music on the weekend.  The Park has several new cabins & great facilities.  We had an excellent ensuite site.  The Don River flows through the Park with a small lake. Next door is a lovely arboretum including a lake full of birds & platypus.
Absolutely enjoyed our stay, Di the caretaker was so lovely and helpful and the amenities was immaculate.  Will be coming back 😁👍

Love staying at this park, the caravan sites are spacious, amenities the cleanest of anywhere we have stayed. Location is just glorious, suits us and our dog greatly.
Caretakers are always going above and beyond to make sure our stay is great. We have had our van for just over a year, and we have been there six times for a minimum of three nights each time. Would be more if we didn't work. It's our go to caravan park because of the care and cleanliness that is put into this place. Love love love Lakeside Caravan Park.

It's a very good camping! The kitchen is full of cutlery, dishes, it has 2 ovens but only 1 fridge, the place is always clean! About the bathroom, it has 7 showers, dunnies and lavatories, so clean as well. The owners are lovely. The wifi work well. I widly recommend it.
Thanks Jacqueline and Simon! Excellent service and attitude. The facilities are in perfect conditions and extremely clean. Highly recommendable

Had a fabulous stay with my family over the school holidays. Close to many interesting locations and attractions. Friendly staff. Great atmosphere, other campers were also friendly and everyone happy to provide info about the local area. Good clean facilities.
Stayed there over the Christmas break. Such a lovely clean quiet park. You are close to so many sights and towns including the Devonport ferry. The owners are helpful and friendly.
Love the quietness, not crowded with too many caravans. You are really one with nature. The camp kitchen and facilities are clean and well equipped.  It's pet friendly.We had a tent.
Great family park. Helpful and friendly hosts.

Very lovely campground. Very clean amenities. Hot showers. Modern, nicely furnished and well equipped camp kitchen and lounge area. Grassy, spacious and level sites. There is a small lake at the back of the campground where you can watch a platypus in the morning and afternoon. The manager was super friendly and helpful. I had an accident and cut myself and she was very caring, provided me with first aid and came back later to check on me. Would highly recommend this campground. Wish we could habe stayed longer.
So lovely and peaceful some lovely walks along the river with the platypus…and the owners are very nice .
Fantastic service and facilities in a gorgeous spot. Highly recommend!

This place is a great find. Well worth the short drive off the main roads to such a serene location. The managers provide a friendly, well presented park. Great even in winter. Wildlife includes platypus.

Such a beautiful relaxing park so close to Devonport (10mins) in lovely parkland.. stream running by with 2 x families of platypuses.  Immaculately clean facilities with a fabulous camp kitchen. Close to Spirit of Tasmania departure area. Surrounded by trees.
Very nice tidy place beautiful cabins
